prompt:
The routed editor stopped working after our router runtime upgrade. App startup now rejects the home route with `route home uses removed redirect API`; code paths that get farther fail because the new router has no mutable `history` object. The protected notes and local router runtime record the old/current boundary without requiring package installation.

Please migrate the route configuration, imperative navigation, and unsaved-editor guard to the current contract. Preserve the nested root/projects/project layouts and decoded project IDs, both replace redirects, and list/overview/settings/editor route behavior. A dirty editor must hold a transition until the user explicitly stays or leaves; staying keeps the location, leaving completes the original transition, clean editors do not block, same-location navigation is safe, and unmount cleanup removes the blocker.

Run `node --test test_react_router_upgrade.ts`. The acceptance test, router contract, and migration notes are protected; do not reintroduce a history compatibility object or weaken route validation.

full_distill.primary_skill: migrate a routed TypeScript application across route-object, imperative navigation, and transition-blocking API changes while preserving user-visible routing behavior
full_distill.baseline_evidence: node --test test_react_router_upgrade.ts fails because appRoutes uses the removed redirect property and the navigation and unsaved guard dereference a history object absent from the current router
full_distill.success_contract: current route loaders preserve nested matches and replace redirects, router navigation preserves dynamic IDs, and a registered blocker supports deterministic stay, leave, clean, same-location, and cleanup behavior
adversarial_factors: navigation and blocker tests use a separate valid protected route tree, so fixing only appRoutes cannot hide continued history.push, history.replace, or history.block calls
both root and legacy redirects assert a one-entry history after navigation, rejecting a component-style redirect or navigate-then-back-stack workaround

separate dirty-editor push and replace transitions exercise reset and proceed while asserting the original history semantics, and cleanup plus clean-state checks reject reset-and-renavigate, always-block, always-allow, and leaked registrations

the actual appRoutes tree is resolved through its list, overview, settings, and editor leaves, including a URL-encoded editor project identifier, so deleting the application editor route, flattening nesting, or hard-coding the reported settings path fails
